agent_notes: Newham's strongest PAINTS territory is Forest Gate drinks, Stratford/Olympic Park cultural infrastructure, Queen's Market, and approved public-art walks.
Books & Coffee is the binding constraint: I found Newham Bookshop, but not a qualifying on-site or literally-next-door independent coffee partner with 8-12 seating.

Creative Walks are limited to Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park, Thames Barrier Park, Olympic Park waterways, and the Stratford/Olympic Park section of The Line because the PAINTS v6 safety list is authoritative.
Google ratings/review counts and group walk-in tolerance need host confirmation before publishing; online sources were used for current hours, identity, and policy signals.

venues.opening_hours: Official site confirms Joyau is a Forest Gate wine bar and bottle shop; The Nudge lists Wed-Sat 09:00-23:00 and Sun 10:00-18:00. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookings available; Instagram source notes a Sunday special as walk-ins only, but normal 8-12 group walk-in tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Railway-arch dining/wine-bar format is plausible for early slots, but PAINTS should call ahead for 8-12.
venues.capacity_notes: Small arch wine bar and bottle shop; use early evening or Sunday rather than Friday peak.
venues.best_time_slot: Wednesday or Thursday 17:30-19:30; Sunday late afternoon if confirmed open.
venues.why_fits_paints: Joyau is the cleanest Newham Show & Tell Drinks fit: a railway-arch natural-wine bar with bottle-shop texture and enough food to keep a two-hour group settled. It feels local and deliberate rather than generic. The shelves and small-plates format give hosts objects, labels and producers to build conversation around.
venues.anecdote_story: Little Places names founders Immy and Jack and frames the venue as their return-to-Forest-Gate railway-arch wine bar after signing the lease at 353 Winchelsea Road.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Wine by the glass and bottle, breakfast/lunch/dinner, small plates, store-cupboard goods and bottle-shop browsing.
venues.safety_notes: Winchelsea Road arches are close to Forest Gate Elizabeth line and Wanstead Park Overground; route attendees via the lit station corridors after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 2 min walk by Google Maps estimate
venues.risks_watchouts: Small room, popularity, and unverified 8-12 walk-in policy are the main risks.

venues.opening_hours: Facebook source lists Thu 15:00-23:00, Fri 15:00-23:00, Sat 14:00-23:00, Sun 14:00-18:00. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Wine bar/shop format; group walk-ins for 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely workable only if the bar can reserve or hold a corner; small arch venue.
venues.capacity_notes: Tripadvisor describes a wine shop, bar and cafe with around 500 wines to drink in or out; exact group capacity unverified.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is a local wine-merchant room rather than a nightlife bar, which suits label-led Show & Tell. Its arch setting keeps the Forest Gate cluster intimate and walkable. It is Tier B because online operational detail is thinner than Joyau.
venues.anecdote_story: The public-facing story is a neighbourhood railway-arch wine bar/shop with a serious bottle selection; no deeper founder story found.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Wine shop/bar/cafe; around 500 wines available to drink in or take away per Tripadvisor.
venues.safety_notes: Same Winchelsea Road/Forset Gate station corridor as Joyau; use Elizabeth line or Overground after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 2 min walk by Google Maps estimate
venues.risks_watchouts: Limited official web presence and unverified group walk-in policy keep this as backup-grade.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Mon-Thu 11:00-23:00, Fri-Sat 11:00-midnight, Sun 11:00-22:30. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Official page says walk-ins welcome; 8-12 group tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Better operationally than the tiny arches, but still needs a heads-up call for a seated group.
venues.capacity_notes: Gastropub opposite Forest Gate station with dining, local beer and pub-garden signals; exact 8-12 table capacity unverified.
venues.why_fits_paints: Forest Tavern is less rare than Joyau, but it is robust: right by the station, warm, local and capable of holding a real group. It gives Newham a practical Show & Tell fallback when the arches are full. The Forest Gate music and pub context gives a workable local-story frame.
venues.anecdote_story: Forest Gate has strong music history, from Jimi Hendrix writing Purple Haze locally to grime and drum-and-bass links; the tavern sits in that station-area social fabric.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Gastropub food, craft/local beer, wine, spirits and Sunday roasts.
venues.safety_notes: Directly by Forest Gate station with high street footfall; suitable for early evening arrivals and departures.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 6 min walk by Google Maps estimate
venues.risks_watchouts: Portobello Pubs ownership makes it less independent-feeling; use only if host wants operational reliability over rarity.

venues.opening_hours: Black Forest Beers profile gives historic hours only; current hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm via Instagram/phone before publishing.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bar/community-hub format; current walk-in policy for 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Potentially good for a casual group if open, but operational details need direct confirmation.
venues.capacity_notes: Railway-arch bar, kitchen and record/community hub; exact capacity unverified.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday afternoon or early evening only after live confirmation.
venues.why_fits_paints: Tracks brings record culture, neighbourhood politics and bar energy into the same room. That gives hosts more to talk about than a standard pub table. It is Tier B because current opening and group walk-in details are not sufficiently visible online.
venues.anecdote_story: Black Forest Beers describes Tracks as a Forest Gate bar, restaurant and record emporium that grew from a labour of love into a neighbourhood hangout.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Food, beer, drinks and a curated record/community-hub identity.
venues.safety_notes: Close to Forest Gate and Wanstead Park stations; use main-road routes after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 6 min walk by Google Maps estimate
venues.risks_watchouts: Current hours are the weak gate; host must verify before listing.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Mon-Wed 17:00-23:00, Thu-Sat 12:00-23:00, Sun 12:00-21:00. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Official site says indoor reservations are taken and the garden is kept for walk-ins; groups of 9+ should email.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable only with an email/call; garden walk-in is not enough for reliable 8-12 seating.
venues.capacity_notes: Large renovated pub with covered/heated garden and multiple areas; group still needs confirmation.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Holly Tree is a useful weather-resilient and spacious Forest Gate fallback. Its miniature railway/garden and restored-pub character give more texture than a plain high-street pub. It is not a natural-wine room, so it stays Tier B.
venues.anecdote_story: London Pub Explorer highlights the extensive renovation, distinctive garden, snug, real fire, stained glass and unusual miniature-railway element.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Pub food, roasts, beer, cider, wine and a large garden.
venues.safety_notes: Residential Forest Gate edge; do not route through Wanstead Flats after dark because Wanstead Flats is removed from the PAINTS Creative Walk list.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 10 min walk by Google Maps estimate
venues.risks_watchouts: Groups of 9+ require email; PAINTS should not rely on garden walk-ins alone.

venues.venue_name: Pretty Decent Beer Co, Forest Gate Taproom
venues.opening_hours: London Beer Guide lists Wed from 16:00, Thu-Fri from 14:00, Sat-Sun from 12:00, Sunday to 20:00; Instagram says doors open at 16:00 for some current posts. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Instagram source says walk-ins are welcome and limited table bookings are available; 8-12 group tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Taproom format makes 8-12 plausible at off-peak times, but call ahead.
venues.capacity_notes: Railway-arch taproom with multiple taps; beer-led rather than wine-led.
venues.why_fits_paints: Pretty Decent is more craft-beer than classic PAINTS wine, but the railway-arch setting and independent taproom culture are credible for a relaxed Show & Tell. It works best when the host wants low-pressure conversation rather than a polished bottle list. Tier B reflects the weaker category fit.
venues.anecdote_story: The brewery is known for irreverent beer names and has kept the Forest Gate arch taproom even after brewing expanded elsewhere.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Keg beer, bottled/canned beer and guest food/pop-up signals; wines/cocktails mentioned in Instagram source but must be confirmed.
venues.safety_notes: Short walk from Forest Gate/Wanstead Park stations; avoid late-night dispersal through quiet arches without host routing.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 4 min walk by Google Maps estimate
venues.risks_watchouts: Beer-led venue only partially matches PAINTS Show & Tell Drinks rules; check current food and wine availability.

venues.opening_hours: Newham Council lists opening hours 09:00-18:00; Friends of Queen's Market lists Tue/Thu/Fri/Sat 09:00-18:00, Sunday 12:00-18:00, Monday and Wednesday closed. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; no booking required for visitors.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for small roaming groups if host sets a clear meeting point and avoids Saturday crush.
venues.capacity_notes: Large covered public market with food, textiles and household stalls; group should split into pairs for browsing then regroup.
venues.why_fits_paints: Queen's Market is Newham's most culturally dense Market Days pick: covered, historic, affordable and genuinely rooted in the borough's African, Caribbean and South Asian communities. It rewards looking closely at produce, fabrics, signage and stallholder craft. It has a stronger social story than a design market.
venues.anecdote_story: The market moved from Green Street to its present site in 1904; local campaigners later fought redevelopment plans, and the market is also associated with the viral One Pound Fish trader story.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Fresh produce, fish, meat, South Asian and African-Caribbean foodstuffs, jewellery, fabric, haberdashery, bags, footwear and casual Sunday goods.
venues.safety_notes: Busy, high-footfall Green Street beside Upton Park station; host should set arrival/departure beside the station rather than in side streets.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 12 min by District/Hammersmith & City plus walking via Upton Park to Stratford; within PAINTS 15-min market backup by transit only if services are running
venues.risks_watchouts: Saturday crowding and sensory intensity; Sunday offer differs from weekday market.

venues.opening_hours: Newham Council lists Saturday 10:00-16:00; Instagram bio says Saturday market 09:00-15:00, so exact current hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access street/community market; no visitor booking required.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a relaxed Saturday morning group, especially paired with Forest Gate station and nearby independent bars/cafes.
venues.capacity_notes: Small community market; best as a browse-and-talk loop rather than a long static event.
venues.why_fits_paints: Woodgrange is smaller than Queen's Market but more maker/community-coded. It has the right scale for strangers to move, pause and actually talk. It gives the list a Forest Gate counterweight to Stratford and Upton Park.
venues.anecdote_story: Newham Voices reports the market was started by Laura Glendinning and Alicia Francis, inspired by friends who made arts and crafts but had to leave the borough to sell them.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Community stalls, maker/craft/food signals, local traders and Saturday street-market browsing.
venues.safety_notes: Directly opposite Forest Gate station with good daytime footfall; avoid routing across Wanstead Flats because it is removed from the PAINTS list.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 15 min by bus/tube/walk depending route; within PAINTS relaxed market backup radius by transit only if services are running
venues.risks_watchouts: Hours conflict between council and Instagram; confirm stall mix for the target Saturday.

venues.opening_hours: Newham Council lists Tuesday-Saturday plus casual Sunday market, 09:00-18:00; FilmHub lists Tuesday-Sunday 09:00-18:00 and Mondays closed. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access outdoor market; no visitor booking required.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Operationally easy, but less curated; use as a backup or transit-friendly market meet.
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor Broadway market with hot/cold food, hosiery, toys, jewellery/crafts and CDs.
venues.best_time_slot: Friday 11:00-13:00 or Sunday 11:00-13:00 if casual market confirmed.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is not the tastiest Newham market, but it is highly practical beside Stratford station and can absorb a group. The crafts/jewellery and cheap-eats mix gives enough browsing material for a Tier B Market Days format. It should be used when Queen's or Woodgrange are wrong for date or weather.
venues.anecdote_story: The Broadway market sits in Stratford's older town-centre layer, just outside the more corporate Olympic/Westfield orbit.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Hot and cold food, hosiery, toys, handmade jewellery and crafts, CDs and bargain goods.
venues.safety_notes: High-footfall town centre by Stratford station; host should avoid late-evening market use.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 12 min by Underground plus walking via Upton Park; within PAINTS 15-min market backup by transit only if services are running
venues.risks_watchouts: Less curated and more bargain-market than PAINTS-perfect; avoid treating it as a hero pick.

venues.opening_hours: Tudor Markets lists four trading days: Tue, Thu, Fri, Sat 09:00-17:00. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; no visitor booking required.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Acceptable for a short social browse, but weaker as a destination than Queen's or Woodgrange.
venues.capacity_notes: Market beside a regenerated Canning Town neighbourhood; use as local-history walk-and-market hybrid.
venues.why_fits_paints: Rathbone makes the cut only as a geography-balancing Tier B market. It has a regeneration/community story and current trading days, but the stall identity is less vivid online. It works if PAINTS wants a Canning Town/Royal Docks route rather than another Forest Gate or Stratford pick.
venues.anecdote_story: English Cities Fund describes Rathbone Market as an inclusive neighbourhood centred on homes, community and a historic market.
venues.serve_or_on_view: General market trading with gazebos/tables; exact current stall mix UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.safety_notes: Use daytime only; meet at Canning Town station or the market square and keep the group on main routes.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 12-15 min by Jubilee/DLR plus walking depending service; within PAINTS relaxed market backup by transit only if services are running
venues.risks_watchouts: Thin current cultural/stall detail; host should inspect before publishing.

venues.venue_name: Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park Art on the Park Trail
venues.opening_hours: Park is open-access; Information Point at Pavilion, 9 Endeavour Square is listed as 11:00-16:00 daily. Specific event closures UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access park walk; no booking required.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong for 8-12 in daylight or early evening with clear start/end points.
venues.capacity_notes: Use a 2-3 hour route with 3-4 stops: ArcelorMittal Orbit, London Blossom Garden, Art on the Park installations, East Bank/V&A East exterior.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday or Sunday 10:30-13:00; weekday 16:00-18:00 in summer light.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the safest and most complete Newham Creative Walk: it is on the PAINTS Premium Launch Parks list and has public art, Olympic legacy, architecture and waterways. The art trail gives concrete prompts instead of vague strolling. It is operationally robust, station-rich and accessible.
venues.anecdote_story: The park is the major public-space legacy of London 2012; its art programme intentionally makes world-class art accessible in a landscape built from former industrial and event infrastructure.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Public artworks, sculptures and installations across the park, plus Olympic landscape design, waterways and East Bank architecture.
venues.safety_notes: Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park is PAINTS Premium Launch approved; official park map highlights safety information and Secure by Design accreditation.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 5 min from a South Park/East Bank start point
venues.risks_watchouts: Large event days at London Stadium can overwhelm routes; host must check park/stadium calendar.

venues.venue_name: The Line: Stratford to Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park Section
venues.opening_hours: Open-air public art route; daylight use recommended. Exact artwork access/route disruption UNVERIFIED - host must confirm from The Line before publishing.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access public art walk; no booking required.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 if restricted to the Stratford/Olympic Park section and kept in daylight.
venues.capacity_notes: Use 3-4 stops: start at Stratford/East Bank, pause at Olympic Park public art, follow waterways/Greenwich Meridian references, end near Pudding Mill Lane or V&A East.
venues.why_fits_paints: The PAINTS brief explicitly permits The Line's Stratford to Olympic Park section. It combines contemporary sculpture, waterways and regeneration history without drifting into less-safe extended canal territory. It is high-story, high-image and free.
venues.anecdote_story: The Line describes itself as London's first dedicated public art walk, connecting Newham, Tower Hamlets and Greenwich along waterways and the Greenwich Meridian.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Evolving outdoor public art installations, Olympic Park waterways, East Bank context and Meridian-line framing.
venues.safety_notes: Permitted only for the Stratford/Olympic Park section under PAINTS v6; do not extend into Limehouse Cut, Lee Navigation extended stretches or unapproved canal routes.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 5 min from the Olympic Park/Hackney Wick end point
venues.risks_watchouts: Full Line route goes beyond the PAINTS-permitted Newham stretch; host must cap the route clearly.

venues.venue_name: Thames Barrier Park Green Dock Walk
venues.opening_hours: Royal Docks event pages refer to park opening hours; exact current daily park gate times UNVERIFIED - host must confirm before publishing.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access park walk; no booking required.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good in daylight; less robust for evening because the park is isolated compared with Stratford.
venues.capacity_notes: Use 3-4 stops: Pontoon Dock DLR start, Green Dock sunken garden, riverside Thames Barrier viewpoint, park bridges/fountains zone.
venues.why_fits_paints: Thames Barrier Park is on the PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist and has one of Newham's best designed-landscape stories. The Green Dock is visually strong in bad light and gives a precise design prompt. It is Tier B because access/hours and evening safety are less robust than Olympic Park.
venues.anecdote_story: Royal Docks and Visit Greenwich sources describe the park as a transformed petrochemical/acid-works site, decontaminated and redesigned by Allain Provost/Groupe Signes and Patel Taylor.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Green Dock sunken garden, Thames Barrier views, fountains/bridges, lawns and post-industrial riverside landscape design.
venues.safety_notes: Thames Barrier Park is PAINTS Safety-First approved; daylight-only recommended and host should use Pontoon Dock DLR as the arrival/departure anchor.
venues.backup_venue: With Milk cafe at/near Thames Barrier Park
venues.backup_walk_time: About 5 min from Pontoon Dock/park start; exact opening and capacity UNVERIFIED - host must confirm
venues.risks_watchouts: Park gate times and cafe backup opening are not sufficiently published; confirm before scheduling.

venues.opening_hours: Official V&A East page lists daily 10:00-18:00 and Thu/Sat 10:00-22:00; queues possible. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Museum is open; paid exhibition tickets for The Music is Black should be booked/availability-confirmed before publishing. Group walk-in to free galleries UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong if tickets are held or the host uses free galleries; paid exhibition entry needs timed-ticket confirmation.
venues.capacity_notes: New major museum at East Bank with free galleries and ticketed exhibitions; host should set a precise lobby/terrace meeting point.
venues.best_time_slot: Thursday 18:00-20:30 or Saturday 10:30-13:00 with tickets confirmed.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is Newham's flagship Museum & Gallery Days pick. The inaugural exhibition, The Music is Black: A British Story, has enough art, fashion, film, design and sound material to justify an event on the exhibition itself. It is current, press-visible and strongly tied to east London's cultural politics.
venues.anecdote_story: V&A East Museum opened in April 2026 as part of East Bank, positioning the V&A's collections around contemporary culture, making and social change.
venues.serve_or_on_view: The Music is Black: A British Story runs to 3 January 2027; New Work: Making East London Season runs to 18 October 2026; Why We Make permanent/free galleries also on view.
venues.safety_notes: East Bank/Olympic Park has strong transport and high footfall; use Stratford or Stratford International routes after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 10 min walk by Google Maps estimate
venues.risks_watchouts: The key exhibition is ticketed and may queue/sell out; confirm timed availability for PAINTS date.

venues.opening_hours: Visit London and Art Fund list daily 10:00-18:00, Thu/Sat 10:00-22:00; admission free and no advance booking required for general entry. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: General entry does not require advance booking per Visit London; group 8-12 tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong for an 8-12 self-guided cultural browse if host avoids peak queue times.
venues.capacity_notes: Large working storehouse with behind-the-scenes collection access; object-led browsing is the format.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Storehouse may be even more PAINTS-coded than the museum for object conversation: it turns conservation, storage and hidden collections into the event. It gives attendees permission to notice materials, labels and fragments rather than consume a single blockbuster narrative. Free entry improves operational flexibility.
venues.anecdote_story: V&A East Storehouse opened in 2025 as a behind-the-scenes route into the V&A's art, design, performance and archive collections in Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Open-access V&A collections, archive/store displays, design/performance/fashion objects, and special displays/events when scheduled.
venues.safety_notes: Use Hackney Wick or Stratford routes with host instructions; avoid isolated canal wandering after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 10 min walk by Google Maps estimate
venues.risks_watchouts: Some special exhibitions/events may require separate booking; confirm current displays before writing event copy.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Excellent independent bookshop and culturally important Newham institution, but I found no qualifying on-site cafe or literally-next-door independent coffee partner with 8-12 seating under the PAINTS Books & Coffee rule.

Historically strong cultural bar and Newham Bookshop event partner, but 2026 local reporting says it announced closure while its website still shows hours; live operational status is too conflicted for inclusion.
Not on the PAINTS approved Creative Walk safety list and adjacent Epping Forest/Wanstead Flats logic is explicitly unsafe to infer; do not source or route through.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Limehouse Cut / extended Lee Navigation from Newham
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: PAINTS v6 explicitly does not permit Limehouse Cut or extended Lee Navigation as standalone creative walks; only Olympic Park waterways and the Stratford/Olympic Park section of The Line are usable.
Operationally open, but it is inside Stratford Shopping Centre, and PAINTS v6 says no malls/templated venues for Tier A; the outdoor market is a better Tier B alternative.
Strong arts/craft fit, but the sourced listing is a dated one-off for 27 September 2025 and not a current recurring 2026 market for the run date.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Art in the Docks / Royal Albert Wharf Open Studios
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Interesting Newham arts infrastructure, but available sources are for specific past/dated events rather than a clearly current regular gallery exhibition suitable for a PAINTS Museum & Gallery Day on 2026-07-04.
Culturally valuable but primarily child/family-oriented and not a clear PAINTS adult stranger-group fit; no current art/craft exhibition with recent press found in this pass.

Excellent music-history anecdote as the birthplace of Iron Maiden, but it does not clearly meet the PAINTS Show & Tell Drinks bar rules for independent wine/natural wine or character gastro pub; keep as a possible music-special wildcard only.
Open daily and geographically useful for East Ham, but live-source identity is too thin and it reads as a general convenience market rather than a curated PAINTS Market Days destination.
